title: RateLimiter::prune() is never called; buckets grow without bound
description: src/ratelimit.rs:51-55 defines prune() to drop stale IP entries, but there is no call site anywhere in the codebase. Every unique IP that hits the inbox endpoint adds a Bucket to the HashMap permanently. Under sustained scanning/probing traffic the map can grow to millions of entries. Fix: spawn a periodic task in server::serve() that calls inbox_limiter.prune() on an interval (e.g., every 5 minutes).
close_reason: P1-P3 fixes: unwrap replaced, INSERT OR IGNORE dedup, X-Real-IP, zeroizing noted, prune wired, strip_suffix, SSRF guard, shared client, dead code removed, issue refs added
